name: sgs/mega-menu
title: SGS Mega Menu
description: Block-based mega menu with template part dropdowns. Works inside Navigation block.

wrapper.label: Wrapper
wrapper._note: The rendered root `<li>.sgs-mega-menu` â€” no spacing/border `supports` declared on this block (`anchor`/`align` are false; no `spacing`/`__experimentalBorder` support), so the layout cluster is deliberately NOT declared here: adding it with no attrMap/prefix would resolve zero members and only worsen the score (ROLLOUT REQUIREMENT 1). Colour maps to WP-native `color` support, which this render.php reads itself (`$attributes['style']['color']`) and emits scoped via `wp_style_engine_get_styles` â€” never auto-inlined (`__experimentalSkipSerialization: true`).

trigger.label: Trigger (link/button, resting state)
trigger._note: The `.sgs-mega-menu__trigger` `<a>`/`<button>`. `linkBgColour` (Spec 35 FR-35-5 fix, 2026-07-21) is a NEW resting-state base for `css:background-color` â€” previously the hover-only `linkHoverBgColour` had no resting counterpart a client could style (STATE_WITHOUT_BASE). Token-fallback pattern (Bean-locked, Option A): style.css's hardcoded `background: transparent` became `background: var(--sgs-mm-link-bg, transparent)`, so an unconfigured instance renders byte-identical. `style.css` also applies the SAME hover colour pair at `:focus-visible` (a11y parity per the states vocabulary's `hover` note) â€” one declared `hover` state covers both CSS realisations. No `font-*`/`text-*` attrs beyond colour exist on this element â€” honest text-cluster gaps.

panel.label: Panel (dropdown)
panel._note: The `.sgs-mega-menu__panel`. `border-top` accent colour + `--sgs-mm-panel-padding` are hardcoded (style.css:158,156) with no attribute behind them â€” honest layout/fill gaps, not omissions. `panelWidth`/`panelAlignment` are variant-selector attrs (layout MODE, not a style member) and surface as ORPHANs via this element's default `panel` prefix, alongside `panelMaxWidth` (claimed above) â€” deliberately left as honest orphans.

badge.label: Badge
badge._note: `badge` (text) and `badgeColour` are real attributes (render.php:75,246-260). `badgeColour` drives the badge background via the scoped `--sgs-mm-badge-bg` custom property (render.php + style.css:128 `background-color: var(--sgs-mm-badge-bg, ...)`), now mapped to css:background-color (Spec 35 vocabulary fix) â€” the `data-sgs-badge-colour` attribute is retained for JS/debugging visibility only.

icon.label: Trigger icon
icon._note: `icon` (Lucide slug) and `iconPosition` (before/after) are selector/scalar controls with no cluster-member equivalent â€” no colour/size override exists for the chevron. Honest zero-cluster element (mirrors `sgs/tabs`' `blockLabel` pattern) so both surface as ORPHANs rather than being invisible.

sgs._ownership_note: OWNERSHIP BOUNDARY (Spec 35): this block's `parent` is `[core/navigation, sgs/adaptive-nav]` (block.json:18-21) â€” it renders ONE `<li>` menu item + its dropdown panel; it does NOT own the surrounding `<ul>`/nav-bar chrome (nav background, item spacing/gap between siblings, mobile-drawer shell). Those belong to the PARENT (`core/navigation`'s own colour/spacing supports, or `sgs/adaptive-nav`'s wrapper). `menuTemplatePart` content rendered inside the panel (via `get_block_template()` + `do_blocks()`) is delegated-rendering â€” its internal blocks own their own styling and are NOT declared here, same boundary as `content-collection`'s card delegation.
